title: Thanks for joining us
content: Check out our explainer on Iran’s military strategy and how it has changed since the June 2025 war. For more on why QatarEnergy’s LNG production halt could shake up global gas markets, click here . You can also read our news story on US Secretary of State Marco Rubio’s remarks on Iran.

title: Here’s what happened today
content: Heavy Israeli strikes targeted Iran’s state TV broadcaster and other areas in Tehran. A commander in Iran’s Revolutionary Guard says the Strait of Hormuz is now “closed” and warned that any vessels attempting to pass through will be attacked. At least six US service members have been killed in action during the conflict with Iran, the US military says. Qatar has condemned Iran’s attacks on its territory and against other Gulf nations in letters to the UN secretary-general and the president of the UN Security Council. US Secretary of State Marco Rubio says the coordinated attacks with Israel against Iran are driven by “self-defence”. Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi says the Kuwaiti government must explain why US jets were over its airspace when the country promised no attacks would be launched from its soil.

title: UAE says it’s dealing with new missile strikes from Iran
content: The United Arab Emirates says it’s working to intercept a major missile attack launched by Iran. “Emirati air defences are currently dealing with a barrage of ballistic missiles coming from Iran,” the Defence Ministry said in a statement. The ministry “affirms its full readiness to deal with all threats to ensure the protection of the country’s territory and the safety of citizens and residents”, it added.

title: Israeli military says it is striking Beirut
content: The Israeli military has said in a social media post that it is targeting Hezbollah facilities in the Lebanese capital. “The IDF began attacking headquarters and weapons depots of the terrorist organisation Hezbollah in Beirut, more details to follow,” the Israeli military said in a social media post. The Al-Manar news channel, affiliated with Hezbollah, reported that Israel was targeting its building in Haret Hreik in Beirut’s southern suburbs.

title: IRIB broadcaster reportedly continues despite Israeli attack
content: The scene is dominated by three elements: the smoke drifting through the air, the lingering echo of several explosions, and, of course, the concern and anxiety among people rushing out of their houses. We know, based on the latest reports, the complex related to IRIB was attacked – the Iranian state broadcaster. We saw thick smoke in footage coming out of that complex. The head of IRIB said they have no update on casualties. We may expect casualties given the scope and the sound of the explosions. The latest update is that the broadcast is ongoing. The former building of the Iranian parliament was also said to be targeted.

title: Explosions heard over Doha
content: In the past few minutes, Al Jazeera’s correspondent has reported hearing multiple explosions in the skies over Doha. Earlier, Qatar, in letters to the United Nations, detailed how its military had intercepted dozens of ballistic missiles and several drones from Iran since the US and Israel launched their attacks on Iran last Saturday. On Monday, Qatar’s air force also shot down two Iranian jets headed towards the country.

title: Markets on edge over Iran’s ‘scorched-earth’ tactic of escalation
content: With strikes and counter-strikes under way throughout the Middle East, and Trump saying the conflict could go on for weeks, at least, analysts are focused on ⁠a long list of imponderables. The scrutiny comes as oil prices jumped over the weekend from $70 to nearly $80 a barrel, and shipping through the critical oil lanes in the Strait of Hormuz ground to a halt. “The conflict with Iran is a wild card, though markets may quickly lose interest if the situation looks likely to devolve from a regional ⁠to an internal conflict,” Tim Duy, chief US economist at SGH Macro Advisors, wrote. In a separate note, SGH president and CEO Sassan Ghahramani – a Tehran native whose father was an Iranian diplomat before the 1979 Islamic revolution – pointed to the uncertainty of the current moment with the possibility of an Iranian civil war. The situation could deteriorate, because of the “scorched-earth tactic of escalation from Tehran to [other] civilian centres… to hit the global economy and pressure an end to the war”, Ghahramani said.

title: Photos: Aftermath of Israeli strikes on southern Beirut
content: Rescue workers carry a body from a building hit by an Israeli strike on the Jnah neighbourhood in Lebanon’s capital [Hussein Malla/AP] The damaged building after the Israeli attack on the area south of Beirut [Hussein Malla/AP] Israeli strikes reportedly killed more than 30 people in Lebanon on Monday [Hussein Malla/AP]

title: Israeli army confirms killing Palestinian Islamic Jihad ‘commander’ in Beirut
content: The Israeli military has confirmed it killed Adham Adnan al-Othman, whom it described as the Palestinian Islamic Jihad commander in Lebanon. The announcement comes after the Quds Brigades, the armed wing of Palestinian Islamic Jihad, said al-Othman was killed last night. In a statement, the Israeli army said he held the position of commander for several years and was responsible for planning attacks against Israel. He recently continued working on behalf of the group inside Lebanon, including training operatives, recruiting members, and procuring weapons, it alleged.

title: Iran denounces US-chaired ‘kids in conflict’ UN meeting after deadly school attack
content: US First Lady Melania Trump has presided over a UN Security Council meeting focusing on children in conflict, days after dozens of children at a school in Iran were reportedly killed in US-Israel strikes. Iranian state media said an air strike hit a girls’ school in southern Iran, killing at least 165 people and wounding dozens more on Saturday. The Israeli military said it was not aware of strikes in the area. The US military said it is looking into the reports. Shortly before Monday’s UN session began, Iran’s ambassador to the UN, Amir Saeid Iravani, said it is “deeply shameful and hypocritical” for the US to convene a meeting on protecting children during war while launching attacks on heavily populated Iranian cities. “For the United States, ‘protecting children’ and ‘maintaining international peace and security’ clearly mean something very different from what the UN Charter provides,” Iravani told reporters. Melania Trump chairs a meeting of the UN Security Council in New York City [Timothy A Clary/AFP]

title: Israeli military says missiles launched by Iran
content: The Israeli military says it identified missiles launched from Iran. In a statement, it said air defence systems are operating to intercept the strikes.

title: Israeli army confirms strikes on Iranian state TV
content: The Israeli air force has begun another attack on Iranian targets under the guidance of Israeli intelligence, according to the military. Fighter jets dropped dozens of munitions and destroyed what the Israeli military said was the Iranian government’s “communications centre”, apparently referring to Iranian TV. The Iranian broadcaster announced earlier that its headquarters in Tehran was under attack. The joint US-Israeli attacks have killed at least 555 people in Iran since the air strikes began on Saturday, according to the Red Crescent.

title: More from US State Secretary Rubio
content: Here are some more remarks from Marco Rubio: The United States will introduce action to mitigate rising energy prices due to a spike in the price of oil caused by the Iran conflict. The US is not postured for ground forces in Iran at this moment, but President Donald Trump has those options. The objective Washington has set can be achieved without ground troops. The hardest hits are yet to come in strikes on Iran.

title: US tells citizens to immediately depart a dozen Middle East nations
content: The ⁠US ⁠State Department has called on nationals to immediately leave ⁠more than a dozen countries in the Middle East during ongoing US-Israeli strikes against Iran. US citizens were urged to leave by commercial means from Bahrain, Egypt, Iran, Iraq, ⁠Israel, the occupied West ⁠Bank and Gaza, Jordan, Kuwait, Lebanon, Oman, Qatar, ⁠Saudi Arabia, Syria, the United ⁠Arab Emirates ⁠and Yemen, according to Mora Namdar, the State Department’s ‌assistant secretary for consular affairs. The intensity of the attacks, the killing of Iranian Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, and the lack of any apparent exit plan have set the stage for a prolonged conflict with far-reaching consequences.

title: ‘Iran would have had nuclear bomb three years ago’: Trump
content: The US president claimed – without any evidence to back his assertion – that had he not scrapped the Iran nuclear deal signed by his predecessor, Barack Obama, Tehran would have had a nuclear bomb by now. “If I didn’t terminate Obama’s horrendous Iran Nuclear Deal (JCPOA), Iran would have had a Nuclear Weapon three years ago. That was the most dangerous transaction we have ever entered into, and had it been allowed to stand, the World would be an entirely different place right now. You can blame Barack Hussein Obama, and Sleepy Joe Biden,” he wrote on his Truth Social platform. Most independent analysts and inspectors had concluded, at the time, that Iran had complied with the JCPOA and kept its uranium enrichment under limit – far below the levels needed to build nuclear weapons.

title: Rubio says US ‘would love’ for Iranians to topple their government
content: Speaking to reporters, the US secretary of state says the US would not “deliberately” target a school, after Iran said 168 people died in an alleged US-Israeli strike. “The United States would not deliberately target a school. Our objectives are missiles, both the ability to manufacture them and the ability to launch them,” Rubio told reporters, while adding that the Pentagon was investigating the alleged incident. He also voiced support for toppling Iran’s authorities, but said it was not the objective of the US-Israel war against Tehran. “We hope that the Iranian people can overthrow this government and establish a new future for that country. We would love for that to be possible,” Rubio said. “But the objective of this mission is the destruction of their ballistic missile capabilities and of their naval capabilities,” he added.

title: Iranian attacks ‘an unacceptable escalation’: Qatar to UN
content: In letters to the UN secretary-general and the president of the UN Security Council, Qatar has condemned Iran’s attacks on its territory and against other Gulf nations, calling them “an unacceptable escalation that threatens the security and stability of the region”. The letters, by Sheikha Alia Ahmed bin Saif Al Thani, Qatar’s ambassador to the UN, also said that the country reserved the right to respond to the attacks. He detailed how, on Saturday, after US and Israeli strikes killed Iranian Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, Iran fired 83 missiles and 12 drones at Qatar. Qatar was able to intercept 81 of those missiles and 11 of the drones, but two missiles slipped through and hit the Al Udeid airbase, where US forces are stationed. One drone managed to strike an early warning system. However, there were no casualties from these events. “A number of missiles were intercepted over multiple civilian areas, including commercial and residential zones such as the West Bay residential area and the Barwa residential city,” the ambassador said in the letters. By the morning of Sunday, March 1, “92 ballistic missiles and 17 drones were detected”, he added. “The State of Qatar renewed its call for an immediate halt to any escalatory actions, a return to the table of dialogue, prioritization of the language of reason and wisdom, and efforts to contain the crisis in a manner that preserves the security of the region, safeguards the interests of its peoples, and prevents slippage toward broader confrontations.”

title: US Embassy in Amman says staff temporarily evacuated after threat
content: The US Embassy in the Jordanian capital, Amman, says it temporarily evacuated its staff because of an unspecified threat. “Out of an abundance of caution, all personnel at the U.S. Embassy have temporarily departed the Embassy compound due to a threat,” it said in a security alert posted on social media. Jordan is among the countries across the Middle East that have been responding to Iranian counterattacks against a joint US-Israeli campaign that killed Iranian Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei. SECURITY ALERT: JORDAN (MARCH 2,2026) Out of an abundance of caution, all personnel at the U.S. Embassy have temporarily departed the Embassy compound due to a threat. pic.twitter.com/cThK21cDTa — U.S. Embassy Amman (@USEmbassyJordan) March 2, 2026

title: Heavy Israeli air strikes pound Tehran
content: Our colleagues at Al Jazeera Arabic say heavy Israeli air strikes have hit multiple parts of the Iranian capital. Strikes targeted areas near a TV network and the central prison complex, as explosions were heard across the city.

title: Israeli military issues evacuation warning in Beirut’s southern suburbs
content: The Israeli military has issued a forced evacuation warning to residents in Beirut’s southern suburbs, specifically from a building identified on a map shared by Israel’s Arabic-language spokesperson. Avichay Adraee said the building and adjacent structures in the Haret Hreik neighbourhood are located near what he claimed were Hezbollah facilities the Israeli military intends to target. He threatened that residents need to evacuate immediately and move at least 300 metres (0.2 miles) away. #عاجل ‼️إلى جميع السكان المتواجدين في منطقة الضاحية الجنوبية وتحديدًا في المبنى المحدد في الخريطة المرفقة والمباني المجاورة له في حي حارة حريك ⭕️أنتم تتواجدون بالقرب من منشآت ومصالح تابعة لحزب الله حيث سيعمل ضدها جيش الدفاع بقوة على المدى الزمني القريب ⭕️من أجل سلامتكم وسلامة… pic.twitter.com/plfDU8sea2 — افيخاي ادرعي (@AvichayAdraee) March 2, 2026

title: Second Kuwaiti sailor killed while ‘carrying out duties’
content: Earlier, we reported that a Kuwaiti sailor was killed while “carrying out duties” with the country’s armed forces. The army has since announced that a second marine has also been killed. It did not provide further details in two separate statements on the circumstances surrounding both deaths. Kuwait has been intercepting drones and missiles launched from Iran in recent days amid Tehran’s retaliatory strikes across the Gulf, following US and Israeli attacks on Iran.

title: Israeli army issues forced evacuation orders in Iran, Lebanon
content: The Israeli military has issued forced evacuation warnings to residents in parts of Tehran and dozens of villages in southern Lebanon ahead of what it says will be attacks targeting military infrastructure. In a post on X, Israeli army Arabic-language spokesperson Avichay Adraee warned people in Tehran’s Evin neighbourhood, particularly near the state broadcaster’s headquarters, to leave the area immediately. He said Israeli forces would operate in the coming hours against what he described as Iranian military infrastructure and warned that remaining in the area would put lives at risk. In a separate statement, Adraee urged residents in the following southern Lebanese villages to evacuate their homes and move north beyond the marked areas: ad-Dhahira (Tyre) Taybeh Naqoura Jebbayn (Tyre) Matmoura Adissa (Marjeyoun) Beit Lif Blida (Marjeyoun) Bani Haiyyan Bint Jbeil Hula Hnein Tayr Harfa Yaroun Kfar Kila Mheibib Meiss el-Jabal Marwahin Maroun al-Ras Markaba Ainatha (Bint Jbeil) Aita al-Shaab Aitaroun Alma ash-Shaab Rab al-Thalathin Ramyah Chihine Tallouseh Khiam He said the military would act against Hezbollah’s activities, and urged civilians to leave the areas “immediately” for their safety.

title: Drones targeting UK base in Cyprus launched from Lebanon: Report
content: An unnamed Cypriot government source says Iranian-made drones that targeted Akrotiri, a UK military base in Cyprus, were launched from Lebanon, probably by Hezbollah. The AFP news agency quoted the source as saying that “it has been confirmed” that the drones, one of which struck a runway, set off from Lebanon. When asked if the Iran-backed group launched them, they replied, “Most likely”. The Akrotiri base, southwest of the sprawling coastal city of Limassol, is one of two bases the UK has retained in the former ⁠colony since independence in 1960. In addition to the military facilities, it houses families of serving personnel. A checkpoint at RAF Akrotiri, a UK sovereign base in Cyprus [File: Yiannis Kourtoglou/Reuters]

title: ‘We knew Iran would hit us after Israel struck them’: Rubio
content: US Secretary of State Marco Rubio has tried to justify the coordinated attacks with Israel on Iran by saying they were driven by self-defence. Faced with questions from reporters on whether the Trump administration needed congressional approval to wage war on Iran, Rubio attempted to frame the US actions as aimed at preempting Iranian strikes against US bases in the region. “If we stood and waited for that attack [from Iran] to come first, before we hit them, we would suffer much higher casualties,” Rubio said. “And so, the president made a very wise decision: We knew that there was going to be an Israeli action. We knew that would precipitate an attack against American forces. And we knew that if we didn’t preemptively go after them before they launched those attacks, we would suffer higher casualties”.

title: Kuwait must explain why US jets were over its airspace: Iran
content: In remarks to state media, Tehran’s top diplomat says the Kuwaiti government earlier announced that its military base would not be used against Iran. “So what were these three fighter jets that were targeted today doing in Kuwaiti territory?” asked Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi, referring to three US aircraft accidentally shot down by Kuwaiti air defences. “Kuwait’s defence system is supplied by the US, and if their claim that Kuwait’s defence system mistakenly shot down three fighter jets is true, they are questioning the prestige of their own army,” Araghchi said. “The Kuwaiti government must explain what American fighter jets were doing there.”

title: Iran says it will set vessels sailing Strait of Hormuz ‘ablaze’
content: A commander in Iran’s Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps (IRGC) says the Strait of Hormuz is now closed and warned that any vessel trying to pass through it would be attacked, according to Iranian state media. “The strait is closed. If anyone tries to pass the heroes of the Revolutionary Guard, the regular navy will set those ships ablaze,” said Ebrahim Jabari, a senior adviser to the IRGC commander-in-chief. The Strait of Hormuz, which lies between Iran and Oman, is one of the world’s most critical oil transit routes, with roughly 20 percent of global oil supplies passing through it. Any disruptions there will likely send crude prices sharply higher and raise fears of a regional escalation. Iranian naval forces take part in a military exercise in the Strait of Hormuz last month [File: Handout/Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps via Anadolu]

title: Photos: Pakistani women mourn death of Ayatollah Ali Khamenei
content: Mourners chant after the death of Iran’s Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei in Karachi, Pakistan [Akhtar Soomro/Reuters] Pakistan called in the military and imposed a three-day curfew in some areas following deadly protests over Khamenei’s killing on Saturday [Akhtar Soomro/Reuters] At least 24 people were killed and dozens were injured in clashes between protesters and security forces across Pakistan on Sunday [Akhtar Soomro/Reuters]

title: Six US troops killed during attack on Iran: CENTCOM
content: At least six US service members have been killed in action after the United States and Israel started bombing Iran on Saturday, the military says. “US forces recently recovered the remains of two previously unaccounted for service members from a facility that was struck during Iran’s initial attacks in the region,” said US Central Command in a statement. “Major combat operations continue. The identities of the fallen are being withheld until 24 hours after next of kin notification.” CENTCOM Update TAMPA, Fla. – As of 4 pm ET, March 2, six U.S. service members have been killed in action. U.S. forces recently recovered the remains of two previously unaccounted for service members from a facility that was struck during Iran’s initial attacks in the region.… — U.S. Central Command (@CENTCOM) March 2, 2026

title: What is Iran’s military strategy? How has it changed since June 2025 war?
content: Iran appears determined to avenge the killing of Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei and other senior Iranian officials following the start of the US-Israeli assault, as Tehran continues to strike back at Israel and US military assets across the Gulf. After Khamenei’s death was confirmed by Iranian state media on Sunday, the Iranian Revolutionary Guard Corps (IRGC) vowed revenge. Iran’s army chief, Amir Hatami, also pledged to continue defending the country. Read more here .

title: Iranian health official says missile struck near hospital
content: A missile detonated near a hospital earlier today, an official at Iran’s Health Ministry says. It’s unclear if there were any casualties or how severe the damage was. In a post on X, Hossein Kermanpour said smoke was seen rising nearby from a missile that landed beside the hospital where he works as a member of the medical staff. “Patients poured out in fear,” he wrote, calling on “all free people of the world to bear witness”. Iranian authorities say several hospitals have been damaged in recent air attacks by Israel and the US, and some facilities have been evacuated. Iranian officials also reported damage to the Aboozar Children’s Hospital in Ahvaz, in western Iran, as well as to three emergency medical centres in East Azerbaijan, Sistan-Baluchistan, and Hamedan provinces. In Tehran, major strikes on Sunday damaged multiple health facilities in two areas, according to videos circulating on social media and material geolocated by Al Jazeera.

title: Israeli army says struck Iran military, intelligence offices in Tehran
content: The Israeli military says it attacked multiple Iranian intelligence offices and other command centres as it continues its attacks on the Islamic Republic. “The [army] struck over 10 headquarters belonging to the Iranian Ministry of Intelligence – the regime’s main intelligence body – as well as numerous Quds Force headquarters,” it said in a statement, referring to the branch of Iran’s Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps (IRGC) that oversees its foreign operations.

title: Iran’s top diplomat says it’s not at war with Gulf neighbours
content: Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi says Iran is “not at war with regional countries” but it is with the United States after the country was attacked on Saturday. Middle East nations must “pressure the US” over its actions against Iran, Araghchi added. He accused the US of “betraying diplomacy” when it carried out air strikes alongside Israel despite ongoing negotiations to avoid a military confrontation. Araghchi suggested US military bases – even if based in Gulf countries – are still legitimate targets. “We’re not attacking our neighbours, we’re attacking US military bases,” he told state media. “US soldiers fleeing to hotels will not prevent them from being targeted.” Iran’s Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi in Geneva, Switzerland, last month [File: Pierre Albouy/Reuters]

title: Trump administration unclear on direction of war on Iran
content: Harlan Ullman, who advises government and business leaders as chairman of the Killowen Group, says the Trump administration in the US doesn’t appear to know what next steps to take in its joint attack with Israel on Iran. “The [Trump] administration does not have a clear-cut strategy for what it is trying to do,” Ullman told Al Jazeera. “Obviously, the impact of the military strikes is to disarm Iran from its weapons systems, its missiles, its ships and its ability to fight. But that is not a strategy, it’s a tactic,” he added. “The original message of ‘regime change’ seems to be going into the distance because how you effectuate a regime change from the air is uncertain and really never has happened before,” said Ullman. The Trump administration appears to be taking a break to “come up with a message that it can present to the American public about what it’s doing in Iran and why it’s doing it and then what happens next”. It will be a challenge for the US to justify the purpose of the war, given that Iran wasn’t posing an immediate threat to the country, said Ullman.

title: Hamas condemns Israeli strikes on Lebanon
content: Hamas has criticised what it described as “aggression” by Israel against Lebanese territory, calling it a “dangerous escalation”. In a statement, the Palestinian group said the air strikes are part of “ongoing violations against Lebanon and its sovereignty” and an extension of what it described as Israeli assaults across the Middle East. Hamas said it holds Israel “fully responsible for the repercussions” and expressed solidarity with Lebanon.

title: Majority of Americans opposed to Trump’s Iran strikes: Snap poll
content: US President Donald Trump’s air strikes against Iran on Saturday are opposed by significantly more Americans than those who support the attacks, a snap poll conducted by the Washington Post concludes. The Post texted 1,003 Americans for their views on the strikes. More than half of the respondents — 52 percent — were either strongly opposed or somewhat opposed to the attacks, in which Iran’s Supreme Leader Ayatollah Khamenei was killed. By contrast, 39 percent said that they strongly supported or somewhat supported the strikes.

title: Israel, US will ensure Iran does not have nuclear capabilities: Israeli UN envoy
content: Israel’s ambassador to the United Nations says his country and the United States will not stop their operations against Iran until their objectives are met and will do everything necessary to ensure Tehran does not have nuclear capabilities. Speaking to reporters in New York, Danny Danon accused Iran of “lashing out in desperation” militarily in response to what he called “decisive and surgical” US and Israeli strikes. The attack on Iran will last “as long as it takes”, he added. Danon also said he believed freedom for the Iranian people “will come sooner than later”. “We hope there will be new Iranian leadership,” he said. Israel’s ambassador to the United Nations, Danny Danon [File: Julia Demaree Nikhinson/Reuters]

title: US army claims it destroyed all Iranian ships in Gulf of Oman
content: A statement by the US Central Command on X says: “Two days ago, the Iranian regime had 11 ships in the Gulf of Oman, today they have ZERO.” “The Iranian regime has harassed and attacked international shipping in the Gulf of Oman for decades,” it added. “Those days are over.”

title: Israeli attacks deepen rift between Hezbollah and Lebanese army
content: Tensions are escalating between Hezbollah and the Lebanese army after the government banned the group’s military activities. As Israeli strikes continued across Lebanon today, Prime Minister Nawaf Salam announced that Hezbollah’s military activities are now officially prohibited, with the cabinet instructing the army to fast-track the group’s disarmament “by any means necessary”. David Wood, senior analyst for Lebanon at the International Crisis Group, warned that any direct confrontation between the army and Hezbollah would have serious consequences. “Some Lebanese Shia soldiers might desert from the army’s ranks or even defect to Hezbollah’s side, causing the armed forces to splinter,” he told Al Jazeera. Hezbollah’s decision to attack Israel following its strikes on Iran risks further destabilising Lebanon at an already fragile moment, Wood added. “For Hezbollah to attack Israel and precipitate a massive military escalation – in Iran’s name – is a pill too bitter for many Lebanese to swallow,” he said, describing the move as “all the more reckless” given Israel’s growing military superiority over the group. Hezbollah was significantly weakened during the 2024 war, in which its longtime leader Hassan Nasrallah was killed by Israel. Since then, Israel has continued to carry out almost daily strikes on southern Lebanon.

title: Why QatarEnergy’s LNG production halt could shake up global gas markets
content: QatarEnergy has suspended liquefied natural gas (LNG) production following a drone attack, straining the global LNG market. On Monday, Iranian drones struck two sites, according to Qatar’s Ministry of Defence: a water tank at a power plant in Mesaieed Industrial City and an energy facility in Ras Laffan belonging to QatarEnergy, the world’s largest LNG producer. While no casualties were reported, QatarEnergy suspended the production of LNG and other products at the impacted sites for security reasons. Qatar’s LNG exports represent 20 percent of the global market. With fewer products reaching the market, LNG supply is down, causing prices to surge. Read more in our detailed explainer on how QatarEnergy’s decision could impact global gas markets.

title: Fresh explosions heard over Doha
content: In the past few minutes, the sounds of fresh explosions could be heard over Doha. The capital of Qatar is among major Middle Eastern cities that Iran has tried to target with its ballistic missiles and drones. Earlier on Monday, Qatar’s air force shot down two Iranian jets headed towards the country, and also intercepted several ballistic missiles and drones. We’ll get you more on the latest explosions when we have it.

title: UN calls for restraint after Israel-Hezbollah clashes in Lebanon
content: UN Secretary-General Antonio Guterres’s spokesman says: “We’re seriously concerned about the exchange of fire across the Blue Line . The situation on the ground is evolving rapidly, and we’re monitoring developments closely.” Attacks on Lebanon reportedly resulted in 31 fatalities and many more injured north of the Blue Line in Lebanon, Stephane Dujarric said. “We urge utmost restraint and call on the parties to uphold the cessation of hostilities agreement,” said Dujarric.

title: Iranian army says it shot down seven more UAVs
content: A statement quoted by the state media says the total number of drones or unmanned aerial vehicles (UAVs) intercepted since the beginning of the war has reached 29.

title: GCC states’ actions will depend on intensity and length of the war: Analyst
content: Abdulaziz Algashian, senior non-resident fellow of the Gulf International Forum, says the Middle East situation is “heading towards some sort of escalation”. “It is unfortunately taking a trajectory that is not very healthy and is concerning,” Algashian told Al Jazeera from Riyadh. The crucial question now is what “the threshold” is for Gulf nations being bombed “to enter the operation and begin to attack Iran”, he said. The likelihood of this happening rises if the war goes on for weeks as US President Trump has suggested, he added. The intensity of Iran’s attacks on Gulf energy infrastructure, which is increasing, is another concern, Algashian said. “Some sort of a GCC-forward defence” is likely to be formed “to try to assert deterrence” if the attacks continue, he said.

title: Trump not ruling out US ground forces in Iran: Report
content: US President Donald Trump says a “big wave” of strikes on Iran is imminent. “We haven’t even started hitting them hard. The big wave hasn’t even happened. The big one is coming soon,” Trump told CNN in an interview, adding the US military is “knocking the crap” out of Iran. “I don’t want to see it go on too long. I always thought it would be four weeks, and we’re a little ahead of schedule,” he said of the war’s potential duration. Trump claimed it’s unclear who is now leading the country after dozens of the Islamic Republic’s officials were killed, including Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei. The president admitted “the biggest surprise” so far in the war has been Iran’s attacks on neighbouring Arab countries: the United Arab Emirates, Qatar, Kuwait, Bahrain, and Jordan. “We were surprised. We told them, ‘we’ve got this,’ and now they want to fight, and they’re aggressively fighting. They were going to be very little involved and now they insist on being involved.” Trump also told The New York Post newspaper on Monday that he’s not ruling out sending US ground troops into Iran “if they were necessary”.

title: Kuwait says naval officer killed in military ‘operation’
content: The Kuwaiti army says a sailor was killed during an “operation” by the Gulf country’s armed forces. The army did not provide details on the circumstances of the death.

title: Travel stocks reel as US-Iran war sparks worst disruption since COVID-19
content: Travel shares have shed $22.6bn as escalating conflict between the US and ⁠Israel and Iran disrupted flights worldwide, closed key Middle ⁠Eastern hubs, and sent oil prices surging. Analysts warn of weeks of disruption. Data showed at least 4,000 flights have been cancelled worldwide in the past three days. The UN’s International Civil Aviation Organization told countries they “have the responsibility to ensure the safety and security of air transport operations, facilities and passengers”. Dubai, the world’s busiest international hub, and Doha remained shut for a third ⁠day, leaving tens of thousands of passengers stranded as aviation faced its biggest test since the COVID-19 pandemic. Oil prices jumped as much as 13 percent to their highest since January 2025 as Iran and Israel stepped up attacks, raising the prospect of higher fuel costs for airlines.

title: Iran’s leaders ‘in denial of reality’ with ‘reckless’ attacks on Gulf states
content: Faisal al-Mudahka, editor-in-chief of the Qatari newspaper Gulf Times, says Iran’s government is “living in a bubble” and in “denial of reality”. He said there appears to be a “disconnection between the political leadership and the revolution”, describing the situation as “very concerning”. Al-Mudahka said three layers of the Islamic Republic’s leadership have been “decapitated” and suggested some remaining leaders may be acting recklessly. Using energy distribution as leverage is “part of Iran’s philosophy”, he said, noting that Tehran has previously sought to disrupt the flow of oil and gas supplies in the Strait of Hormuz – one of the world’s most strategically important trade routes.

title: Turkiye’s Erdogan calls for ‘end to the bloodbath’ in Iran
content: The Turkish president has promised to help reach a ceasefire between the United States and Israel and Iran. “We are on the side of peace. We want an end to the bloodbath, for the tears to stop flowing, and that our region finally reach the lasting peace it has cried out for for years,” Recep Tayyip Erdogan told members of his party in Ankara. Erdogan, who maintains good relations with US President Donald Trump despite frequently criticising American ally Israel, insisted the United States-Israeli strikes on Saturday, which sparked the war and triggered retaliation from Tehran, were “illegal”. “We will intensify our contacts at all levels until a ceasefire is concluded and tranquility is restored to our region,” he said, adding that he is “grieving at the sight of the suffering of the civilians and innocent children” in Iran hit by the conflict. Muslim-majority Turkiye, which is a member of NATO, shares a 500km (315-mile) border with Iran. Turkish President Recep Tayyip Erdogan [File: Emin Sansar/Anadolu Agency]

title: Iran denounces US-Israel attacks on schools and hospitals
content: The Iranian president has condemned strikes on schools and hospitals by the US and Israeli forces. “Attacks on hospitals strike at life itself. Attacks on schools target a nation’s future,” President Masoud Pezeshkian said in a post. “Targeting patients and children blatantly violates humanitarian principles. The world must condemn it. Iran will not remain silent or yield to these crimes,” he added. Attacks on hospitals strike at life itself. Attacks on schools target a nation’s future. Targeting patients and children blatantly violates humanitarian principles. The world must condemn it. I stand with my grieving nation. Iran will not remain silent or yield to these crimes. — Masoud Pezeshkian (@drpezeshkian) March 2, 2026

title: Israeli strikes on Lebanon kill 52 people, wound 154 others
content: Israeli strikes on Lebanon have killed at least 52 people and wounded 154 others, the Lebanese government said in an updated toll. An earlier casualty toll shared by the Health Ministry said 31 people were killed and 149 wounded. The bombings of southern and eastern Lebanon and Beirut’s southern suburbs have also displaced more than 28,500 people, according to the government’s disaster and management unit. Smoke billows following Israeli bombardment of Beirut’s southern suburbs on Monday [AFP]

title: Drone hits fuel tank terminal in Abu Dhabi, situation contained
content: A drone has struck a fuel tank terminal in the Emirati capital Abu Dhabi, causing a fire although operations have not been impacted. “Abu Dhabi authorities have responded today to a fire resulting from the targeting of a Musaffah fuel tank terminal by a drone,” the Abu Dhabi Media Office said in a statement. “The situation was promptly contained. No injuries were reported and there was no impact on operations.”

title: Iran’s IRGC attacks oil tanker in Strait of Hormuz
content: Iran’s Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps (IRGC) says it attacked an alleged US-linked oil tanker in the Strait of Hormuz as part of a wave of strikes retaliating against the US-Israel assault. “The ATHE NOVA tanker, one of the American allies in the Strait of Hormuz, is still on fire after being hit by two drones,” the IRGC said in a statement. On Saturday, the elite paramilitary force said it closed the waterway, which is vital for global oil-and-gas shipments, after the start of US and Israeli attacks. The Strait of Hormuz handles roughly one-fifth of the world’s traded oil and significant volumes of liquefied natural gas exports from Qatar and the United Arab Emirates. About 20 percent of global daily oil consumption — roughly 20 million barrels — passes through the narrow corridor.

title: NATO chief says no plans for bloc to join war on Iran
content: NATO Secretary-General Mark Rutte has praised US and Israeli military action against Iran but has ruled out the NATO alliance joining the Middle East conflict. Speaking to Germany’s ARD television in Brussels, Rutte said it is “really important” what the US is doing “together with Israel” to degrade Iran’s capacity to develop nuclear and ballistic missile capability. NATO as an alliance won’t be involved, however. “There are absolutely no plans whatsoever for NATO to get dragged into this or to be part of it,” Rutte said, adding that individual allies may support US efforts.

title: Erdogan tells NATO’s Rutte, Germany’s Merz that diplomacy is needed in Iran conflict
content: Turkish President Recep Tayyip Erdogan has told NATO Secretary General Mark Rutte in a phone call that diplomacy must be given an opportunity to achieve lasting and sustainable peace in the Middle East, according to the Turkish presidency. The presidency said that issues on NATO’s agenda, as well as regional and global developments, were discussed during the call. The office added that Erdogan told Rutte that Ankara was closely following the conflict between Iran, Israel and the United States. Separately, Erdogan held talks with German Chancellor Friedrich Merz regarding the conflict. According to his office, Erdogan told Merz that the conflicts in the region adversely affect regional and global security, reiterating his call for the parties to resume negotiations.

title: UK lets US use bases for Iran clash: A military, legal quagmire
content: Early on Monday, a suspected Iranian drone crashed into the runway at the United Kingdom’s RAF Akrotiri base in southern Cyprus. British and Cypriot officials said the damage was limited. There were no casualties. Hours later, two drones headed for the base were “dealt with in a timely manner”, according to the Cypriot government. But the drone attack has raised legal questions about the UK’s role in the regional conflict, as many legal experts argue the US violated international law following its strikes on Iran. Read more here .

title: Israeli army reports new attack from Lebanon
content: Alerts have been activated in several Israeli areas near the border with Lebanon because of hostile aircraft, according to a military update. The details are under investigation, the Israeli army added.

title: Hezbollah slams Lebanese government’s ‘impotence’ towards Israel
content: The group has condemned the country’s government after Lebanese Prime Minister Nawaf Salam announced that Hezbollah’s military and security activities are banned. Salam’s announcement came hours after Israel responded to the Iran-linked group’s rocket and drone attack by launching major air raids on the southern suburbs of Beirut. “We understand the Lebanese government’s impotence in the face of the brutal Zionist enemy, which violates national sovereignty, occupies land, and poses a continuous threat to the country’s security and stability,” Hezbollah said, adding that it is the government ‘s right “to decide on war and peace”. It added: “However, given this clear weakness and deficiency, we see no justification for Prime Minister Salam and his government to take such aggressive measures against the Lebanese who reject the occupation.” The group’s statement also said Hezbollah’s reaction to Israel is “the way to achieve the illusory Lebanese security and peace”.

title: Middle East war putting civilians ‘in grave danger’, Red Cross says
content: The unfolding war in the Middle East is endangering civilians as attacks intensify, says Mirjana Spoljaric, head of the International Committee of the Red Cross (ICRC). “Widening hostilities across the Middle East are putting civilian lives in grave danger,” Spoljaric said in a statement. “The scale of major military operations flaring across the Middle East risks embroiling the region – and beyond – into another large-scale armed conflict that will overwhelm any humanitarian response.” "Widening hostilities across the Middle East are putting civilian lives in grave danger. The scale of major military operations flaring across the Middle East risks embroiling the region – and beyond – into another large-scale armed conflict that will overwhelm any humanitarian… pic.twitter.com/0sALYjy5yH — ICRC (@ICRC) March 2, 2026

title: Photos: Civilians flee following Israeli air attacks on Lebanon
content: Displaced people flee Israeli attacks in southern Lebanon [Mohammed Zaatari/AP Photo] A displaced family rests in Dahiyeh, in southern Beirut [Bilal Hussein/AP Photo] A girl sits in a van as displaced families arrive in the southern port city of Sidon [Mohammed Zaatari/AP Photo]

title: Trump says US military acting with ‘ferocious, unyielding resolve’
content: US President Donald Trump has honoured the troops killed in Iranian attacks in the Middle East. “Today we grieve for the four heroic service members killed in action and send our love and support to their families,” said Trump. “In their memory we continue this mission with ferocious, unyielding resolve to crush the threat this terrorist regime poses to the American people.”

title: Iran war could go longer than four weeks: Trump
content: In his rambling speech, Trump also has referred to his comments in an interview with the Daily Mail on Sunday in which he said the military campaign against Iran could last up to four or five weeks. In fact, he said, the US was prepared to go longer if needed, adding that he wouldn’t get “bored” if the war dragged on.

title: ‘You can’t deal with these people’: Trump says Iran backed out of deals twice
content: The US president has blamed the administration of his predecessor Barack Obama for allowing Iran to develop its nuclear programme – a main justification he used for launching the war. “They were on the road to getting one [a nuclear weapon] legitimately through a deal signed foolishly by our country,” said Donald Trump. “Finally, we’re ensuring the regime cannot fund or direct armies outside of their borders. We thought we had a deal, and then they backed out. Then they came back, we thought we had a deal, and they backed out. I said, ‘You can’t deal with these people’. We gotta do it the right way.” Oman, which was mediating the indirect talks between the US and Iran, has however said that Tehran did make major concessions on its nuclear programme — it was willing to stop stockpiling enriched uranium, and had agreed to dilute its existing enriched uranium dramatically. The two countries were “close” to a deal, Oman said, when the US and Israel attacked Iran. President Donald Trump speaking before participating in a Medal of Honor ceremony [Mark Schiefelbein/AP Photo]

title: Italy says evacuating hundreds of citizens from UAE
content: Italy says it has been helping to evacuate hundreds of its citizens from the United Arab Emirates, estimating there are currently around 30,000 Italian nationals in the country. Speaking in the Italian Senate, Foreign Minister Antonio Tajani said a group of 98 Italians had left the UAE for Oman yesterday and were due to arrive in Rome later today. Another group of around 200 Italian students who had been stranded in Dubai are due to be evacuated on a UAE flight to Milan on Tuesday. “We are creating corridors and networks of embassies to facilitate the movement of Italians to countries where there are flights available,” Tajani said. He said Italy would help with charter flights to bring back thousands of Italians who were due to transit through Dubai on their way back from the Maldives. A plume of smoke caused by an Iranian strike is seen in the background as Emirates planes are parked at Dubai International Airport after its closure in Dubai, the United Arab Emirates on Sunday, March 1, 2026 [Altaf Qadri/AP Photo]

title: Trump says ‘everyone was behind us’ in attack on Iran
content: The US leader has justified the joint American-Israeli strikes on Iran, saying the country’s nuclear programme was a growing threat to the United States. “This was our last best chance to strike and eliminate the intolerable threats posed by this sick and sinister regime,” US President Donald Trump told the media in the East Room of the White House. He said the “objectives are clear”, with the first goal to destroy Iran’s missile capabilities. “Second, we’re annihilating their navy; we’ve already knocked out 10 ships. They’re at the bottom of the sea. Third, we’re ensuring that the world’s number one sponsor of terror can never obtain a nuclear weapon,” said Trump. US President Donald Trump speaks about Iran before a Medal of Honor ceremony in the East Room of the White House in Washington, DC, the United States [Alex Brandon/AP Photo]

title: Israeli military says it carries out strike in Beirut
content: The Israeli military confirms on X that it has carried out a “targeted” attack in Beirut without ⁠providing further ⁠details.

title: Iranian govt urges people to rally in Tehran to support Islamic Republic
content: Iran’s government has urged people to gather this evening across the capital to show support for the Islamic Republic during ongoing attacks. The rally is also to pay tribute to Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei , who was killed on Saturday in US or Israeli air strikes. The appeal was broadcast on state television on the third day of joint US-Israeli attacks on the country.

title: Trump about to speak from the White House
content: The US president is set to address the media in Washington, DC. US President Donald Trump attends a Medal of Honor ceremony [Jonathan Ernst/Reuters]

title: How much do Israel and the US care about civilian lives in Iran?
content: Our colleagues at AJ+ have spoken to journalist Jeremy Scahill, of Drop Site News, about the US-Israeli attacks on Iran. Watch his interview below: How much do Israel and the U.S. care about civilian lives in Iran? pic.twitter.com/LWVw48z8pI — AJ+ (@ajplus) March 2, 2026

title: Israeli army says people can leave shelters after reported Iranian attack
content: The military’s Home Front Command says people can leave bomb shelters in areas where there were hostile aircraft alerts. The army previously reported it was intercepting missiles launched from Iran. The military update advised people to stay close to shelters throughout the country. At least 10 Israelis have been killed and dozens wounded in retaliatory strikes after Israel and the United States launched attacks on Iran on Saturday.

title: Palestinian Islamic Jihad says senior commander killed in Israel’s Lebanon strikes
content: Al-Quds Brigades, the group’s armed wing, has announced that a senior commander was killed in an attack targeting the southern suburbs of Lebanon’s capital, Beirut, overnight. The group identified him as Adham al-Othman, 41, and described him as the “commander of al-Quds Brigades in the Lebanese arena”. Meanwhile, the Israeli army claimed that it struck more than 70 targets belonging to Lebanon’s Hezbollah group in its attacks. Israel said that it started its strikes after Hezbollah fired rockets at northern Israel overnight.

title: Explosions hit Beirut with plumes of smoke billowing into the sky
content: Huge plumes of smoke are rising into the sky of the Lebanese capital. The strikes took place in the southern suburbs of Beirut. We’ll bring you more soon…

title: Editor’s Choice: What to read and watch right now
content: We’ve published a number of pieces covering all aspects of the conflict in recent hours: Iran demands international action after attacks impact hospitals, schools Gas prices soar as QatarEnergy halts LNG production after Iran attacks Israel intensifies war on Lebanon after Hezbollah attack What is Iran’s military strategy ? How has it changed since June 2025 war? Why killing Khamenei isn’t the death of Iran’s Islamic Republic And there’s plenty more here .

title: Iran-aligned group claims 23 drone attacks on US sites in Erbil
content: The so-called Islamic Resistance, an umbrella group aligned with Iran, has claimed responsibility for 23 drone attacks targeting the US consulate and the airport in Erbil. These Iran-aligned groups say the attacks are in relation to the killing of Iran’s supreme leader. Three additional drones were reportedly launched by the groups and were intercepted. The developments are being described as highly dangerous, despite politicians calling for calm. Authorities in Iraq have warned against using Iraqi soil to launch attacks against any of the parties involved in the fighting, cautioning that such actions could drag Iraq further into the conflict.

title: Photos: Aftermath of Israeli air attacks on Dahiyeh, Beirut’s southern suburbs
content: Smoke rises from Israeli air attacks on Dahiyeh [Hassan Ammar/AP Photo] [Hassan Ammar/AP Photo] [Hassan Ammar/AP Photo]

title: Iranian MP says nine hospitals attacked by Israel and US
content: Fatemeh Mohammad Beigi, a member of parliament’s health commission, says five of the attacked hospitals are in Tehran and four are in other cities, according to state media. She added that Gandhi Hospital in Iran’s capital has been completely evacuated, its patients moved to other institutions. A damaged room inside Gandhi Hospital in Tehran [Abedin Taherkenareh/EPA]

title: Amnesty calls for urgent probe into killings during Iran protests in Pakistan
content: Amnesty International has called for a “thorough, independent, impartial and effective” investigation into the killings of at least 26 people during protests that broke out across Pakistan following the killing of Khamenei. “The killing of protesters and reports of the use of lethal ammunition by security forces against these assemblies raises serious concerns about unlawful use of force,” Babu Ram Pant, Amnesty’s deputy regional director for South Asia, said in a statement. “Protesters have a right to gather peacefully and express dissent, even in moments of heightened tension. During such crises, security forces must demonstrate their commitment to protecting life and safeguarding peaceful assembly,” he added.

title: ‘We are not joining US-Israeli offensive’ on Iran, UK PM Starmer says
content: The UK prime minister says that while he has allowed British bases to be used in the US response to Iran’s attacks, they will be “limited to the agreed defensive purposes”. “We are not joining the US-Israeli offensive strikes. The basis for our decision is for the collective self-defence of longstanding friends and allies and protecting British lives,” Starmer said. He added that while the UK will not be joining the strikes, “we will continue our defensive actions in the region.”

author_tagline: Reporting from Ramallah, occupied West Bank
title: Constant state of emergency in Israel is putting ‘significant pressure’ on the population
content: In the afternoon, Israel faced six waves of Iranian missiles. The attacks spanned a very large area, covering much of Israeli territory and parts of the occupied West Bank, from Ashkelon on the southern coast all the way to Netanya, as well as areas of the Negev. Sirens were going off across these regions, followed by reports of impacts, including near Tel Aviv’s Ben Gurion Airport, where a building was struck by shrapnel. In Ashkelon, most of the injuries were light, with only one person described as being in moderate condition. However, the sustained state of emergency, with constant sirens and repeated missile alerts, has placed significant pressure on the population, keeping people in or near shelters and severely disrupting daily life. In the occupied West Bank, where there are no public shelters, schools have shifted to online instruction for at least the coming week. Meanwhile, the Israeli government and military are speaking of a conflict that could last for weeks. Officials have discussed striking targets in Iran in ways that they claim could destabilise the government and create what they describe as an opportunity for internal unrest. While such outcomes have not materialised, this rhetoric helps explain the waves of Israeli bombardment targeting Tehran and other cities across Iran.

title: US-Israel trying to ‘blunt, degrade’ Iran’s weapons systems
content: A former operations director of the US military’s Central Command says the goal of destroying Iran’s missile and drone arsenal is “very achievable” along with “whatever enrichment capability is left” in the country. “What’s playing out now is a discussion on how long it will take to do that,” Kevin Donegan told Al Jazeera. He said military operations such as this are extremely complex, noting the “friendly-fire” incident today in which Kuwaiti defence systems shot down three US fighter jets because of “misidentification”. “Those are inherent risks in any military operation,” Donegan said. “The focus on the United States and Israelis now is to blunt or degrade – as quickly as they can – the offensive capabilities of the Iranians to keep wreaking havoc. You want to stop these attacks or at least diminish them as much as you can.” Smoke rises after a reported Iranian strike near the US embassy in Kuwait City [AFP]

title: Starmer says Iran stepping up ‘reckless’ strikes after Khamenei’s killing
content: The British prime minister says the killing of Khamenei has led Iran to strike military and economic targets in a more “reckless” manner in the region. The death of Khamenei “will not stop Iran from launching these strikes”, Starmer told parliament. “In fact, their approach is becoming even more reckless and more dangerous to civilians,” he added.

title: Trump claims 49 Iranian leaders killed, ‘big wave’ of strikes yet to come
content: The US president has said a “big wave” of US military action against Iran is still to come, describing the current campaign as only the beginning of a broader assault. “We haven’t even started hitting them hard. The big wave hasn’t even happened. The big one is coming soon,” Trump said in an interview with CNN. On the duration of the conflict, Trump said, “I don’t want to see it go on too long. I always thought it would be four weeks. And we’re a little ahead of schedule.” The president also said the “biggest surprise” had been Iran’s attacks on Bahrain, Jordan, Kuwait, Qatar and the United Arab Emirates. He also claimed 49 Iranian leaders were killed in initial strikes.

title: Qatar says it shoots down 2 Iranian fighter jets
content: Qatar’s Defence Ministry says its air force has “successfully shot down” two fighter aircraft incoming from Iran. Naval and air defence systems also took down seven ballistic missiles and intercepted five Iranian drones, the ministry said in a statement. “The threat was addressed immediately upon detection, in accordance with the operational plan, as all missiles were shot down before reaching their targets,” it said. pic.twitter.com/BMQysv6o8C — وزارة الدفاع – دولة قطر (@MOD_Qatar) March 2, 2026

title: UK PM Starmer says he ‘stands by’ decision not to take part in Iran strikes
content: UK Prime Minister Keir Starmer has defended his decision to keep Britain out of the US and Israel’s initial strikes against Iran after criticism from Trump. “President Trump has expressed his disagreement with our decision not to get involved in the initial strikes, but it is my duty to judge what is in Britain’s national interest. That is what I have done, and I stand by it,” Starmer told parliament. “But it has become clear that Iran’s outrageous response has become a threat to our interests and our allies, and it cannot be ignored.” The UK leader also said British military bases in Cyprus “are not being used by US bombers” after he agreed to a US request to use its bases for “specific and limited defensive purposes” in the war.

title: Oil prices rise, financial markets fall in response to Middle East conflict
content: We’re seeing oil prices rise while the markets, in general, are down on the news of what’s happening in the Middle East. Oil prices of course are going to go up when you see a disruption in the supply chain like what we’re seeing right now. A fifth of all oil consumed in the world comes through the Strait of Hormuz, and that transportation location is now under threat. We’re seeing the benchmark for stocks in oil, the Brent crude oil stock, up nearly 8 percent to almost $80 a barrel now, and the concern is that that could reach $100 a barrel. We’re seeing OPEC countries rally to try to prevent that from happening. They’re vowing to produce 200,000 more barrels a day to offset the rising prices, but if they can’t deliver that oil, then prices are likely to rise. Rising oil prices could affect other goods and services, as well as the financial industry, so that’s why we’re seeing not a dramatic decrease in the market today, but certainly a decline of less than one percent in all the major stock indexes here as volatility and nervousness permeate the trading floor today.

title: Photos: Aftermath of US–Israeli air raids on Tehran
content: A destroyed police station in Tehran after it was struck amid the US–Israeli military campaign [Vahid Salemi/AP Photo] Civil defence workers survey the rubble of a destroyed police station [Vahid Salemi/AP Photo] [Vahid Salemi/AP Photo]

title: Israel’s largest airport to reopen in ‘extremely limited format’
content: Israel’s main international gateway, Ben Gurion Airport near Tel Aviv, says it expects to reopen this evening in what it describes as an “extremely limited format”. In a message posted on its WhatsApp channel cited by Reuters, the airport said flight operations would gradually expand from Tuesday, depending on the security situation. Only Israeli carriers are expected to resume flights initially. El Al Israel Airlines said it was seeking approval to operate charter flights from Europe to destinations bordering Israel to help return passengers stranded abroad. The carrier said it was planning flights from more than 20 cities, including New York, Miami, Los Angeles, Bangkok, London, Paris and other European destinations, to bring back an estimated 40,000 passengers.

title: Israeli army says it is intercepting new wave of Iranian missiles
content: The Israeli military reports that missiles have recently been launched from Iran towards Israel, and its defence systems are working to eliminate them. “In recent minutes, the Home Front Command distributed advance instructions directly to mobile phones in the relevant areas,” it said in a statement. “The public is asked to be responsible and act according to the instructions.”

title: Iran claims to have struck Israeli army’s communications complex in Beersheba (Bir as-Sab)
content: Iran’s Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps (IRGC) says the 11th wave of Iranian missiles and drones targeted Israel’s Beersheba (Bir as-Sab) city, according to the Tasnim news agency. The report claimed the Israeli army’s communications industries complex was hit in the city. Later, the agency posted a photo of what it claimed was a building targeted in Bir as-Sab’, “which houses major international companies, including Microsoft”. إعلام العـ,دو : إيران قصفت المجمع العلمي في بئر السبع، الذي تقع فيه كبرى الشركات العالمية، ومن ضمنها شركة ميكروسوفت. pic.twitter.com/TqiGzcsD9G — وكالة تسنيم للأنباء (@Tasnimarabic) March 2, 2026 Translation – Enemy media: Iran bombed the scientific complex in Beersheba [Bir as-Sab], which houses major international companies, including Microsoft.

title: Iran ‘focusing on hurting the United States’ with Gulf attacks
content: When asked by Al Jazeera whether it’s a good idea for Iran to be making enemies of its Gulf neighbours with its attacks, Hassan Ahmadian, of the University of Tehran, said “not at all … I don’t think it’s a rational choice”. However, Iran is actually “focusing – as the official line goes – on US forces, on US bases and basically US interests in the region. And those interests seemingly, as we see in the attacks, include global oil and gas markets,” said Ahmadian. Iran is “focusing on hurting the United States in the way that can hurt many in the region as well”, he told Al Jazeera. Iran is targeting energy infrastructure in Gulf countries to “ratchet up the pressure to force the United States to rethink its aggression against the country through driving up prices of oil and energy prices globally”, added Ahmadian.

title: Iranian commander vows to bomb Cyprus to force US aircraft to leave
content: Brigadier General Ibrahim Jabari, a commander of Iran’s Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps (IRGC), has been quoted by the Tasnim news agency as saying that Iran plans to launch missiles at Cyprus. “The Americans have moved most of their aircraft to Cyprus, and we will launch several missiles at Cyprus to force them to leave from there as well,” he said, according to the report.

title: Macron says France will increase nuclear warheads amid global uncertainty
content: French President Emmanuel Macron has announced that his country will increase the number of nuclear warheads in its arsenal for the first time in decades. Speaking at the L’Ile Longue military base in northwestern France, home to the country’s ballistic missile submarines, Macron said he had decided to raise the number of warheads, now at fewer than 300. He did not specify how many would be added. It would mark the first increase in France’s nuclear stockpile since at least 1992. Macron said the move was aimed at clarifying the role of France’s nuclear deterrent in Europe’s security architecture amid what he described as growing global threats and uncertainty surrounding US security guarantees to European allies. Macron also said France would allow the temporary deployment of its nuclear-armed aircraft to allied countries and announced that France, Germany and the UK would work together on “very long-range missile projects” as part of deeper cooperation on strike capabilities.

title: Lebanon closes all private higher education institutions and Lebanese University for a day
content: Rima Karami, Lebanon’s education minister, has announced that all private higher education institutions and Beirut’s Lebanese University will be closed on Tuesday, according to local media. Karami was quoted as saying that the decision came “ due to the evolving security situation resulting from the Israeli aggression against several Lebanese regions”.

title: Loud explosions reported in area near Isfahan nuclear facility, airbase in central Iran
content: We’re getting reports of loud explosions being heard from the area near a nuclear facility and airbase in the central Iranian city of Isfahan. We’ll bring you more updates as soon as we get them.

title: Berlin to evacuate German children, pregnant women from Middle East
content: The German government is preparing to evacuate vulnerable nationals stranded due to the conflict in the Middle East, including children, sick people and pregnant women, Foreign Minister Johann Wadephul says. Aircraft will be sent to the Saudi capital, Riyadh, and Muscat in Oman, Wadephul said. Up to 30,000 tourists travelling with German travel agencies are believed to be affected by the escalating regional conflict, which began on Saturday with US-Israeli strikes on Iran. Tehran has since retaliated, attacking US forces in Gulf states, such as Qatar and the United Arab Emirates that also serve as key international air transport hubs. A spokesman for the German Defence Ministry had said earlier in the day that evacuations would be a “last resort”. Wadephul said the Federal Foreign Office would send crisis support teams to Muscat, Doha and Dubai while “further teams are investigating the situation at the border crossings”. German nationals could be encouraged to cross borders into countries such as Oman and Saudi Arabia whose airspace is still open. A team from the German embassy in Cairo is assisting with the border crossings from Israel.

title: Bahrain says air defence systems intercepted new wave of Iranian missiles, drones
content: Bahrain says its air defence systems have intercepted a new wave of Iranian ballistic missile and drone attacks. “The total number of hostile projectiles amounted to 70 missiles and 59 drones,” the National Communication Centre said, according to state media.

title: Italy says it receives requests for logistics help and supplies from Gulf countries
content: ⁠Italy ⁠has received requests for logistics support ⁠and supplies from Gulf ⁠countries caught up in the conflict between Iran and ‌Israel and the US, according to Italian Foreign Minister Antonio Tajani. Addressing ⁠a parliamentary ⁠committee in Rome, Tajani also said Italian ⁠soldiers deployed ⁠in Lebanon, ⁠Jordan and other parts of the region were ‌safe.

title: Israel’s forced evacuation orders trigger ‘mass displacement’ in Lebanon
content: The Israeli army has threatened to carry out a series of air strikes across numerous areas in Lebanon, singling out, for example, every branch of al-Qard al-Hasan, a financial institution affiliated with Hezbollah, and saying it will target all of its branches across the country. There is widespread fear in Lebanon that this could escalate into days of sustained violence. There was smoke rising from Beirut’s southern suburbs, a cluster of neighbourhoods that are now largely empty. Since the early hours of the morning, the hundreds of thousands of residents who live in this area have fled. Israel is clearly maintaining pressure, saying it has launched an offensive against Hezbollah and expects several days of fighting. Officials say they intend to intensify and expand the strikes, warning that Hezbollah will “pay the price” for firing a barrage of rockets across the border in the early hours of the morning, which Hezbollah described as retaliation for the killing of Iran’s supreme leader, Ali Khamenei. When Israel issues forced evacuation orders, it effectively triggers mass displacement. Families are forced to leave quickly and seek shelter in safer areas. That, in itself, creates another humanitarian crisis. We’ve been on the streets since around 3am, watching families pack whatever they can and leave. There was a steady stream of vehicles heading out. We’ve seen people sitting in their cars along the roadside, searching for shelter in schools, mosques or hotels. In southern Lebanon, the Israeli army has ordered evacuations in dozens of towns and villages near the border. Many of those communities are now largely empty. People are afraid. While Israel sometimes issues advance warnings before strikes, several of the attacks in the past few hours appear to have come without warning. Often when that happens, the targets are specific individuals.

title: Russia, UAE reiterate call for ceasefire in US-Israeli conflict with Iran
content: Russia’s Foreign Ministry says President Vladimir Putin and United Arab Emirates President Mohammed bin Zayed Al Nahyan have reiterated the need for an immediate ceasefire between Iran and the US and Israel during a phone call. According to a Russian statement, the two leaders discussed what Moscow described as “tragic developments” in the Middle East. Both sides emphasised the importance of returning to political and diplomatic efforts. The Russian statement described recent military action against Iran as an “unprovoked act of armed aggression against a sovereign UN member state”. The UAE president, according to Moscow, said Iran is attacking his country “despite the fact that UAE territory is not being used as a launchpad for attacks against Iran and [the Iranian attacks on the UAE] are therefore unjustified”.

title: US expects strikes to ‘encourage internal unrest’ or ‘regime change’ in Iran
content: Hegseth says the US “didn’t start the war”, framing the operation as retaliation for actions attributed to the Iranian government since the 1979 revolution. He cited several incidents, including Iran’s support for proxy groups and the alleged use of improvised explosive devices in Iraq that killed US soldiers. The stated US objectives are to degrade Iran’s naval forces, ballistic missile capabilities and nuclear infrastructure. US officials described the air operation as highly successful so far. However, there is no clear timeline. Although Trump suggested it could last up to four weeks, his defence secretary declined to commit to a specific duration, saying it would not be an “endless war”. There also appears to be an expectation, at least rhetorically, that pressure from these strikes could encourage internal unrest and potentially regime change. However, experts note that regime change would likely require a significant ground presence – potentially hundreds of thousands of soldiers – rather than air operations alone. Meanwhile, opposition groups inside Iran remain unarmed and unsupported, and recent protests have already resulted in thousands of deaths, raising concerns about further crackdowns. So while US officials say they have clear military objectives – eliminating Iran’s missile capacity, neutralising its navy and preventing it from obtaining a nuclear weapon – it remains unclear how those goals translate into political outcomes on the ground in Iran.

title: Child among 5 civilians killed in Israeli attack on western Iran: Report
content: Five civilians, including a child, have been killed in an Israeli bombing on the village of Sarabeleh in western Iran’s Kermanshah province. That’s according to Iran’s semiofficial Tasnim news agency.

title: US urges citizens not to travel to Lebanon, to leave immediately
content: The US embassy in the Lebanese capital of Beirut has called on its citizens to not travel to the country. It has also urged those already there to “depart Lebanon NOW while commercial flight options remain available”, it said in a social media post. “The security situation in Lebanon is volatile and unpredictable. Airstrikes have occurred throughout the country, especially in the south, the Beqaa, and parts of Beirut.” We urge U.S. citizens not to travel to Lebanon. If you are in the country, depart Lebanon NOW while commercial flight options remain available. The security situation in Lebanon is volatile and unpredictable. Airstrikes have occurred throughout the country, especially in the… — U.S. Embassy Beirut (@usembassybeirut) March 2, 2026

title: Iraq now ‘directly affected by course of war’, foreign minister says
content: Iraqi Foreign Minister Fuad Hussein says his country is now “directly affected by the course of the war”, warning its continuation would exacerbate instability and create chaos in the region, according to the Iraqi News Agency. Hussein spoke by phone with British Minister of State for the Middle East Hamish Falconer, and they discussed the developments in the war and its regional repercussions, the agency said. The call included “a comprehensive assessment of the field and political situation” and Falconer reviewed his country’s new position, explaining its decision “allowing US forces to use some [UK] facilities”, the news agency reported. Hussein stressed “the necessity of intensifying international and regional efforts to reach an immediate ceasefire, which would contribute to containing the escalation and creating the conditions for restoring stability”, the report said.

title: Israeli military releases further details on Hezbollah commander’s killing
content: The Israeli military has released additional details about the killing of a senior Hezbollah figure in Beirut. Earlier, the army said it had killed Hussein Makled, described as the head of Hezbollah’s intelligence headquarters, in an overnight strike on the Lebanese capital. In a statement, the Israeli military said Makled had held several positions within Hezbollah’s intelligence apparatus over recent years and was appointed to lead the unit after his predecessor, Hussein Hazima, was killed in October 2024. According to the Israeli military, Makled was responsible for compiling intelligence assessments on Israeli forces and coordinating with senior Hezbollah commanders to plan attacks against Israel. The military added that it would continue operations against Hezbollah.

title: Emirates says limited flights to begin this evening
content: Dubai-based carrier Emirates has said it will begin operating a limited number of flights starting this evening. “We are accommodating customers with earlier bookings as a priority, and those who have been rebooked to travel on these limited flights will be contacted directly by Emirates,” it said in a statement, urging people to “not go to the airport unless you have been notified”. It added: “All other flights remain suspended until further notice.”

title: Latest casualty figures
US mission in Iraq tells nationals, personnel to shelter in place
content: The US embassy in Baghdad says it has told US nationals and mission personnel to shelter in place, warning of threats posed by Iran-backed groups active in the country. “The US Mission in Iraq advises US citizens to exercise increased caution, maintain a low profile, and shelter in place until further notice,” it said in a statement, adding that it had directed all staff to do the same. The embassy warned that “Iran-aligned terrorist militias continue to pose a significant threat to public safety”. Yesterday, hundreds of pro-Iran Iraqi protesters clashed with security forces near the embassy, with authorities accusing demonstrators of firing live rounds that wounded security personnel. It had been their second attempt to enter the area after clashing in the morning with heavily deployed security forces. Security Alert – U. S. Embassy (Baghdad, Iraq) (March 2, 2026) Location: Iraq, Countrywide Event: U.S. Mission Iraq advises U.S. citizens to exercise increased caution, keep a low profile, and shelter in place until further notice. U.S. citizens should avoid crowds, as… — U.S. Embassy Baghdad (@USEmbBaghdad) March 2, 2026

title: Israel says its forces begin striking Hezbollah ‘throughout Lebanon’
content: Israel’s army says its forces have begun striking “Hezbollah terror targets throughout Lebanon”. “Further details to follow,” it said in a statement published on Telegram as large plumes of smoke were rising over Beirut.

title: Massive explosions rock Lebanon’s Beirut
content: Huge plumes of smoke are rising into the sky of the Lebanese capital. We’ll bring you more soon.

title: Caine issues warning to US ‘enemies’
content: The general has issued a warning for US “enemies”. “To those who would test our resolve or threaten the United States or its allies, understand clearly: We can reach you. We can sustain the fight, and we can scale the fight, and we will prevail.”

title: Hegseth declines to rule out potential future deployment of US ground forces in Iran
content: The US defence secretary has reiterated that there are currently no US “boots on the ground” in Iran but declined to rule out such future steps. He added that Washington would go as far as necessary to advance US interests while also saying it would act without being “foolish”. Hegseth questioned the logic of publicly outlining potential military options, saying it would not make sense to inform an adversary of what actions the US might take in pursuit of its objectives.

title: Russia’s Putin expresses solidarity with Qatar in call with emir after Iranian attacks
content: Qatar’s emir, Sheikh Tamim bin Hamad Al Thani, has had a phone conversation with Russian President Vladimir Putin. According to a statement from the emir’s office, Sheikh Tamim thanked Putin after the president expressed Russia’s solidarity with Qatar following Iran’s attacks and its readiness to provide any support or assistance. The two leaders also discussed regional developments and stressed the importance of intensifying diplomatic efforts to reduce tensions and maintain regional security and stability.

title: US has achieved ‘air superiority’ over Iran: Caine
content: US forces have achieved air superiority over Iran, Caine says during the news conference. “[Strikes by US forces] resulted in the establishment of local air superiority. This air superiority will not only enhance the protection of our forces but also allow them to continue the work over Iran,” the general said.

title: Wife of Iran’s killed supreme leader succumbs to injuries: State media
content: The wife of Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei, who was injured in the same US-Israeli attack that killed her husband, has succumbed to her injuries, according to Iran’s Tasnim news agency.

title: Hegseth gives details on US casualties
content: The US defence secretary is providing further details about the US casualties during operations against Iran, saying most incoming threats had been intercepted by air defence systems. He said that while the majority of projectiles had been shot down, one strike had penetrated defences and hit what he described as a fortified “tactical operations centre”, noting that the weapons used were “powerful”. As we’ve reported, the US military has said four of its service members have been killed in the operations.

title: Caine lays out objectives of US-Israel operation on Iran
