content: Footage from Beirut shows fire and smoke in the skies after Israel bombs the Lebanese capital. Watch below:

title: ‘Heavy exodus’ from southern Lebanon to Beirut
content: The Lebanese National News Agency is reporting “massive displacement” in the south of the country following the exchange of fire between Israel and Hezbollah. It said there is a “heavy exodus” from the Tyre region towards Beirut and northern Lebanon. It said civil defence teams are directing traffic. As we’ve been reporting, the Israeli military has ordered the residents of at least 53 villages and towns in southern Lebanon to evacuate their homes.

title: Hezbollah enters the fray. Why now?
content: For more than 15 months, Hezbollah has observed a de facto one-sided ceasefire, absorbing near-daily Israeli attacks without responding. The status quo symbolised the decline of the group that was once considered a fearsome regional force. Yet, the group was not ready to wave the white flag. It projected defiance and rejected calls to disarm. Now, it has struck back – not only in response to the Israeli violations, but to put what remains of its capabilities on the Iranian side of the scale in the ongoing regional war. Iran has been Hezbollah’s patron, acting as its main funder and weapon supplier. Without Tehran, it would be difficult for Hezbollah to rebuild its arsenal, pay its fighters or run its social programmes. So the fight for the survival of the Iranian ruling system is an existential matter for Hezbollah. The Lebanese group may also be looking to alter the current balance of power that practically allows Israel freedom of military action in Lebanon. But it’s unclear whether it can hurt Israel or meaningfully help Iran in the ongoing conflict. The group’s opening strike consisted of a few rockets that did not result in any casualties. Hezbollah has lost thousands of fighters since the start of Israel’s genocidal war on Gaza in October 2023. Its military wing is believed to have been significantly degraded. And it is dealing with a Lebanese public that is weary of war and crises. Hezbollah domestic opponents, including Prime Minister Nawaf Salam, are already decrying the attack on Israel, saying it will only embolden Israeli violations against Lebanon.

title: Qatar changes strategy on Iranian attacks
content: Qatar has shifted away from what I would call its initial phase. Over the past 48 hours, we’ve seen the Patriot defence system being deployed to intercept these missiles and drones. But if you intercept these projectiles, there are risks from falling debris. So now, the Defence Ministry is saying that they are deploying fighter jets to shift from intercepting missiles over the city of Doha to shooting them down over the waters of the Gulf. We’ve been standing here in central Doha for the past 10 hours, and we haven’t heard any explosions. In fact, the explosions seem a bit distant now and far away, compared to the situation on Saturday.

title: Poll suggests only quarter of Americans support attacks on Iran
content: A Reuters Ipsos poll has found only that one in four respondents approve of the US-Israeli attacks on Iran. Some 43 percent of respondents said they disapproved of the war, and another 29 percent said they were unsure. Approval among Republicans was stronger, but not resounding, with 55 percent saying they approved of the US-Israeli strikes,13 percent disapproved, and 32 percent were unsure. Most significantly, about 42 percent of Republicans said they would be less likely to support the operation if it led to “US troops in the Middle East being killed or injured”. Read more here .

title: ‘Continuous explosions’ reported in Tehran
content: The Nour News outlet is reporting “continuous explosions” in various areas in the east and west of the Iranian capital. We’ll bring you more when we have it.

title: More US lawmakers to join effort to curb Trump’s authority to attack Iran
content: US Senator Adam Schiff has said he will join other lawmakers in forcing a vote on a war powers resolution that would curb Trump’s authority to strike Iran. “Congress holds the power to declare war – not the President,” Schiff said in a post on X. “I will be joining Senators @SenTimKaine, @SenRandPaul and @SenSchumer in forcing a vote on our war powers resolution to make it clear: Congress has not authorized this use of our military.” Such a resolution, if passed by the US House of Representatives and the US Senate, would require the president to seek congressional approval for future strikes. But Trump could veto such a resolution, unless it has two-thirds support in both chambers of Congress, a likely unreachable barrier. Under the US Constitution, only Congress can declare war.

title: UK reports suspected drone strike on military base in Cyprus
content: The UK’s Ministry of Defence has said that a suspected drone has hit its military base at Akrotiri in Cyprus. It said there were no casualties and that the strike occurred at around midnight. The base at Aktokiri is the UK’s main military facility for its operations in the Middle East, and the raid comes after Prime Minister Keir Starmer said his country had agreed to a US request to use the UK’s bases for attacks on Iran.

title: Iran’s Araghchi calls for ‘immediate, concrete, and effective measures’ from UN
content: Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi has sent a letter to UN Secretary-General Antonio Guterres and the UN Security Council decrying the killing of Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei in US and Israeli strikes. The letter decried Khamenei’s killing as a “cowardly act of terror” and a “dangerous and unprecedented escalation that strikes at the most fundamental norms of statehood and civilized conduct among nations”. “Such conduct does not merely violate established principles of international law; it recklessly opens a dangerous Pandora’s box, eroding the bedrock of sovereign equality and the stability of the international system,” he said. Araghchi said Iran “solemnly calls upon the Secretary-General of the United Nations and the Security Council to discharge their Charter-mandated responsibilities for the maintenance of international peace and security and take immediate, concrete, and effective measures to ensure the full accountability of the United States and the Israeli regime for the aforementioned atrocious terrorist act”.

title: Videos show people fleeing Beirut
content: Footage, verified by Al Jazeera, shows heavy traffic in Beirut as residents flee the Lebanese capital in the wake of Israeli attacks. Watch below: View this post on Instagram View this post on Instagram

title: Pentagon chief to hold news conference on Monday
content: The 8am local time (13:00 GMT) news conference by Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth will be the first time a top Trump official has faced the media since the US and Israel launched attacks on Iran on Saturday. Hegseth is set to appear alongside Joint Chiefs of Staff Chairman John Caine. The Pentagon said the officials will discuss the attacks on Iran, dubbed “Operation Epic Fury”. Trump has given several interviews since the strikes, and released two video speeches, but has not held a news conference.

title: Photos: Iraqi security forces fire tear gas on protesters near US Embassy in Baghdad
content: A person waving an Iranian flag walks near tear gas fumes, as Iraqi security forces fire gas at protesters as they try to approach a bridge leading to the Green Zone, where the US Embassy is located in Baghdad, on March 1, 2026 [Ahmad al-Rubaye/ AFP] A protester holds a Kataib Hezbollah flag as supporters of Iraqi Shia armed groups attempt to move towards the US Embassy in Baghdad’s Green Zone [Ahmed Saad/ Reuters] Demonstrators run amid tear gas near Baghdad’s Green Zone [Ahmed Saad/ Reuters]

title: Israel orders residents of dozens of Lebanese villages to leave their homes
content: The Israeli military has issued forced evacuation orders for people in 53 villages and towns in southern Lebanon, including the town of Bint Jbeil. The warning for such a vast area mirrors the mass displacement orders Israel issued during its genocidal war on Gaza.

title: Israeli raids hit 3 towns in southern Lebanon
content: Our correspondents are reporting Israeli strikes in the towns of Haris, Nabatieh al-Fawqa, and Mefdoun. This comes after the Israeli military said it was attacking Hezbollah after a rocket attack on northern Israel.

title: Israel says it targeted senior Hezbollah figures in Beirut
content: The Israeli military says it struck senior Hezbollah figures in the Beirut area, and that it also hit another “central” person from the Lebanese armed group in southern Lebanon. We’ll bring you more as soon as we get it.

title: Japan’s Itochu says fuel shipments from Gulf impacted by attacks on Iran
content: Japanese ⁠trading house Itochu has said it is experiencing “some impact” on shipments ⁠of its crude oil and petroleum products from ‌the Gulf, according to the Reuters news agency. It said this was because several ports there have been affected by fallout from the US and Israeli attacks on Iran. Itochu ⁠said it will respond ⁠by sourcing supplies from regions outside the ‌Middle East. About 20 to 30 percent of global seaborne oil shipments travel through the Strait of Hormuz, which separates Iran from the Arabian Peninsula.

title: US, Bahrain, Jordan, Kuwait, Qatar, Saudi Arabia, UAE issue joint statement
content: The seven countries say they strongly condemn Iran’s “indiscriminate and reckless missile and drone attacks” across the Middle East. They said these “unjustified strikes” have “targeted sovereign territory, endangered civilian populations, and damaged civilian infrastructure”. Tehran’s actions represent a dangerous escalation that threatens regional stability, the countries said, adding that they stand united in the defence of their citizens. They also firmly reaffirmed their right to self-defence.

title: Hezbollah may be seeking to preempt Israeli attacks
content: Ali Rizk, a Beirut-based security analyst, says Hezbollah still “possesses quite a formidable arsenal” despite the pounding it received from Israel during the genocidal war on Gaza. Rizk told Al Jazeera that some Hezbollah officials have been expecting Israel to target leaders of the armed group after the attacks on Iran. “Hezbollah believes that it’s going to be next on the list,” Rizk said. The group may also be testing Israel’s ability to handle a new front in the war, he said. Smoke rises after Israeli strikes in Beirut’s southern suburbs, Lebanon, March 2, 2026 [Mohamad Azakir/ Reuters]

title: Video shows fireballs, smoke in Tehran’s skies
content: As we’ve been reporting, Israel has announced it launched a new wave of attacks on the Iranian capital. Footage, verified by Al Jazeera, shows balls of fire lighting up the sky in western Tehran, followed by sounds of explosions and clouds of black smoke. author_tagline: Reporting from Ramallah, the occupied West Bank
title: Hezbollah attack opens another front in ongoing war
content: We now have confirmation from Hezbollah that these projectiles were sent in retaliation for the killing of the Iranian supreme leader, after Israel and the US started their attack on Iran early on Saturday. Of course, that breaks the ceasefire that was in place since November 2024. We know that Israel continued attacking Hezbollah on Lebanese soil since the ceasefire started, but Hezbollah, on its end, had said it had remained committed to the ceasefire. We are entering the third day of fighting, and yet another front has been opened, in Lebanon this time.

title: Lebanese PM calls Hezbollah rocket attack on Israel ‘irresponsible’
content: Nawaf Salam says the attack was “an irresponsible and suspicious act that jeopardises Lebanon’s security and safety and provides Israel with pretexts to continue its aggression”. “We will not allow the country to be dragged into new adventures, and we will take all necessary measures to apprehend the perpetrators and protect the Lebanese people,” the prime minister said on X. Hezbollah claimed responsibility for the attack, saying that it was in response to the killing of Khamenei and Israeli violations against Lebanon.

title: Iranian media say 20 killed in attack on Tehran’s Niloofar Square
content: We’ve heard the sound of fighter jets and a massive explosion in Tehran. We are also getting reports of explosions in eastern and western Iran, and there are fears that it’s not just military headquarters that are being targeted, but also civilian residential areas. The Mehr news agency is reporting that more than 20 people have lost their lives in an attack on the Niloofar Square in Tehran. This shows the scope of the attacks on Iran, with raids targeting not just political centres and military headquarters. It all started in the Pasteur neighbourhood, where the offices of the supreme leader and the president are located. We are witnessing damage to civilian buildings, with some of them fully demolished in some cases. And this is concerning because the civilian fatalities are growing. One official has come out to say that 180 children have been killed at the girls’ school in Minab in southern Iran.

title: Anger on Pakistan streets after US and Israel kill Khamenei
content: Protests erupted across Pakistan on Sunday, with 20 people dead and dozens wounded nationwide, after the United States and Israel confirmed the killing of Iran’s supreme leader , Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, in coordinated air strikes on Tehran. The deaths included 10 people in Karachi , at least eight in Skardu and two in the capital, Islamabad, as demonstrations largely led by members of Pakistan’s Shia Muslim community escalated and security forces fired tear gas and rubber bullets at protesters. Pakistan, a country of more than 250 million people, is predominantly Sunni Muslim, but Shia account for more than 20 percent of the population and are spread across the country. In Islamabad, thousands gathered near the Red Zone, the heavily fortified district that houses the parliament, government offices and foreign embassies. Protesters chanted, “Those who side with the US are traitors” and called for “revenge against Israel”. Read more here .

title: Death toll from Israeli attack on Iran girls’ school rises to ‘about 180’
content: Hossein Kermanpour, the head of public relations at Iran’s Health Ministry, says the Israeli attack on the girls’ school in Minab on Sunday has killed “about 180 young children”. He added that the “same type” of missile was used to attack the Gandhi Hospital in Tehran hours ago.

title: Israel bombs Lebanon’s Beirut
content: Our colleagues at Al Jazeera Arabic are reporting Israeli attacks in the southern suburbs of Beirut after Hezbollah launched attacks on northern Israel. We’ll bring you more when we have it.

title: Kuwait shoots down a ‘number of hostile aerial targets’
content: The Kuwaiti Army says its air defences have “confronted and intercepted” a “number of hostile aerial targets at dawn today” in the central parts of the country.

title: Israeli military says responding ‘forcefully’ to Hezbollah strikes
content: Israel has said it is striking Hezbollah “throughout Lebanon” in response to a rocket barrage fired by the group. In a statement, it said the military will not allow Hezbollah to “pose a threat to [Israel] or harm residents of the north”. It pledged to respond “forcefully to this attack”.

title: White House says Pentagon briefed congressional staff
content: White House spokesman Dylan Johnson has told reporters that the Pentagon briefed “bipartisan staffs of several national security committees in both chambers” of the US Congress about the attacks on Iran. “Secretary Rubio, Secretary Hegseth, Director Ratcliffe, and Chairman Caine will brief the full membership of both chambers of Congress on Tuesday, March 3,” Johnson said. The update comes as lawmakers, particularly Democrats, have called for a vote on a resolution to rein in Trump’s ability to commit military actions without congressional approval.

title: Israel intercepts rocket from Lebanon
content: The Israeli military has said it intercepted rockets launched from Lebanon and that the projectiles caused no injuries or damage. Footage, verified by Al Jazeera, shows streaks of light in the sky as the rockets are launched.   View this post on Instagram   A post shared by صفحة أخبار بيروت (@saf7et.a5bar.beirut)

title: Israel launches more strikes on the ‘heart of Tehran’
content: Israel’s military has said it has launched another wave of attacks on Iran. “The Air Force, guided by military intelligence, has now launched another wave of strikes toward targets of the Iranian terror regime in the heart of Tehran,” it said in a post on X.

title: Hezbollah claims responsibility for attack on Israel
content: The Lebanese group says it targeted an Israeli base in Haifa in retaliation for the killing of Iran’s Khamenei, “in defence of Lebanon and its people and in response to the repeated Israeli aggressions”. “The resistance leadership has always affirmed that the continuation of Israeli aggression and the assassination of our leaders, youth and people gives us the right to defend ourselves and respond at the appropriate time and place,” the group said in a statement. “The Israeli enemy cannot continue its fifteen-month-long aggression without a warning response to halt this aggression and withdraw from the occupied Lebanese territories.”

title: Bahrain says 1 dead in fire from debris of intercepted missile
content: Bahrain’s Interior Ministry says that falling debris from an intercepted missile caused a fire on a “foreign ship” in the Salman industrial zone. “The fire resulted in the death of an Asian worker and serious injuries to two others,” it said in a statement. “The fire has been brought under control and extinguished.”

title: What are Iran’s weapons as it fights US and Israel?
content: After the United States and Israel launched joint strikes on Iran on Saturday, killing Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei and several senior officials, Tehran was quick to respond. Iran said its retaliation targeted Israel and US-linked military sites across the region, including in Gulf states that host US forces. Central to Iran’s response is its missile arsenal and the other platforms and tools at its disposal to inflict pain on the US and others. From ballistic missiles with a range of up to 2,000km (1,243 miles) and cruise missiles that can travel up to 2,500km (1,553 miles), drones, and the threat of making the Strait of Hormuz hard to use, Iran has several arrows in its quiver. Read our detailed explainer on what Iran’s options are.

title: Trump’s objectives still not clear
content: There hasn’t been much clarity about exactly what will lead to the end of this particular operation. The US president has just arrived back at the White House. He emerged from the helicopter, didn’t speak to the media, and went straight inside. We are told that there aren’t any plans to stage a formal delivery to the American people, but we do understand that the secretary of state, the secretary of defence, the director of the CIA, and the chairman of the joint chiefs of staff will be briefing members of Congress on Tuesday. Democrats have already pointed out the fact that the deaths of US soldiers announced earlier today is a consequence of Trump’s actions; a consequence, they argue, of his not involving Congress in the decision to start this conflict. There will also be senior members of his MAGA movement – who have always been incredibly loyal – who may be pointing fingers and saying that ‘you were the president who said you didn’t want soldiers dying in foreign countries, and yet you are the president who has now done that’. US President Donald Trump waves as he returns to the White House after Israel and the US launched strikes on Iran, in Washington, DC, US, March 1, 2026 [Nathan Howard/ Reuters]

title: Iranian attack sets off fire near Bahrain’s Mina Salman Port
content: Bahrain’s Ministry of Interior says “the Iranian aggression” has targeted a maritime facility near the Mina Salman port in Manama. It said civil defence forces are “initiating its procedures to control the fire”. Footage of the incident, verified by Al Jazeera, shows firefighters trying to put out flames as huge columns of smoke billow from the site. عاجل بالفيديو | العدوان الإيراني يستهدف إحدى المنشآت البحرية بالقرب من ميناء سلمان والدفاع المدني يباشر إجراءاته للسيطرة على الحريق pic.twitter.com/0WKFV7suFG — صحيفة البلاد البحرينية (@albiladpress) March 1, 2026

title: Debris from Iranian missile injures 4 in Syria
content: A father and his three daughters were injured when debris from an Iranian missile fell on the town of Ain Terma near Damascus in Syria, the country’s state-run SANA news agency said. The four were taken to hospital, where they were treated for what the news agency said were minor injuries.

title: Explosions rock US base near Baghdad airport
content: Footage posted online, verified by Al Jazeera, shows columns of smoke rising from inside the US’s Victoria Base near Iraq’s Baghdad international airport. Iraqi media, citing security sources, reported that two drones targeted the base, with one falling inside and another being shot down. We’ll bring you more when we have it. انفجارات تهز قاعدة فكتوريا قرب مطار بغداد pic.twitter.com/rnuQW5pDxC — واحد عراق 🇮🇶 (@iraqwan1) March 1, 2026

title: Australia says no plans to join attacks against Iran
content: Australian Foreign Minister Penny Wong told the country’s Nine News on Monday that Canberra has no plans to join the US-Israel military campaign against Iran. “We didn’t participate in these strikes, and we wouldn’t anticipate participating in the future,” Wong said. Australia has had a tense relationship with Iran. In 2025, it expelled the Iranian ambassador to Canberra after accusing the Iranian Revolutionary Guard of orchestrating anti-Jewish arson attacks in Sydney and Melbourne – allegations that Tehran rejected. In the past, Australia has been part of US-led military campaigns, including in Iraq in 2003.

title: WATCH: Trump threatens Iran with unprecedented force as US confirms military deaths
content: US President Donald Trump has ⁠warned that combat ⁠operations against Iran will continue until “all objectives are achieved”. He also confirmed in a video posted on ⁠Truth Social that ⁠three US troops were ⁠killed and ⁠said ⁠there would likely be more American casualties. “We have three, but we expect casualties. But in the end, it’s going to be a great deal for the world,” Trump told NBC News after the Pentagon announced that three unidentified US service members were killed and five were seriously wounded. Trump said the operation, which has killed Iran’s supreme leader and dozens of other top government and military figures, is “ahead of schedule”. Watch our video report below:

title: A recap of recent developments
content: United States President Donald Trump has promised to avenge the three American soldiers killed in Iranian attacks, and warns that more casualties could come. Israeli police say nine people have been killed and 11 are missing as rescuers search for survivors after an Iranian missile attack on the central city of Beit Shemesh . Trump has urged Iranians to rise up and topple the Islamic Republic, a day after a joint US-Israeli attack killed the country’s supreme leader, Ayatollah Ali Khamenei , and up to 40 security officials. UK Prime Minister Keir Starmer says he has accepted a request from the US to use his country’s military bases for “defensive” strikes against Iran. Countries in the Gulf have pledged to defend themselves against Iranian attacks, including by “responding to the aggression”, as Tehran continues to attack US assets in the region. Iran’s retaliatory strikes have hit Qatar, Bahrain, Jordan, Oman, Kuwait, Saudi Arabia and the United Arab Emirates, with attacks reported on airports, residential buildings and hotels.

title: Welcome to our coverage
content: Hello, and welcome to our live coverage of the US and Israel’s attacks on Iran. Stay with us for the latest developments, analyses and reactions from the US, Israel, Iran and the wider region. For updates from Sunday, March 1, go here . Smoke rises following an explosion after Israel and the US launched strikes on Iran, in Tehran, on March 1, 2026 [Majid Asgaripour/WANA via Reuters]
